headline: JPMorgan Stake Highlights Ventas Valuation Debate And Senior Housing Outlook
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has disclosed a significant passive institutional stake in Ventas, Inc. (NYSE:VTR). The position adds a large global financial institution to Ventas, Inc.'s roster of major shareholders. The disclosure highlights fresh institutional interest in the healthcare real estate investment trust sector. For you as an investor, the key point is that a major institution has chosen to commit meaningful capital to Ventas, Inc., a healthcare focused real estate investment trust...

headline: Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan, TD Securities, Morgan Stanley, and Bank of America Join LTX in Bid to Unlock Greater Liquidity in Corporate Bonds
summary: LTX, an AI-powered corporate bond e-trading venue backed by global Fintech leader, Broadridge Financial Solutions, Inc. (NYSE:BR), today announced that Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan, TD Securities (through its subsidiary, TD Financial Products LLC), Morgan Stanley, and Bank of America have joined LTX as fully integrated liquidity providers. This major milestone underscores the participants' commitment to serving buy-side clients by delivering increased choice and improving liquidity in fixed income

headline: Citi's new profit target underwhelms at bank's investor day
summary: At the major bank's investor day on Thursday, Citigroup (C) announced new guidance on return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) that ultimately underwhelmed Wall Street. Market Catalysts Anchor Julie Hyman reports on the investor reaction to Citi's forecasts and new $30 billion share buyback program.

content.title: Citi's new profit target underwhelms at bank's investor day
content.description:
At the major bank's investor day on Thursday, Citigroup (C) announced new guidance on return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) that ultimately underwhelmed Wall Street.

Market Catalysts Anchor Julie Hyman reports on the investor reaction to Citi's forecasts and new $30 billion share buyback program.
content.summary: At the major bank's investor day on Thursday, Citigroup (C) announced new guidance on return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) that ultimately underwhelmed Wall Street. Market Catalysts Anchor Julie Hyman reports on the investor reaction to Citi's forecasts and new $30 billion share buyback program.

headline: Umicore - Transparency notification by JP Morgan Chase & Co.
summary: Transparency notification byJP Morgan Chase & Co. In accordance with article 14, § 1 of the law of 2 May 2007 on the disclosure of major holdings, Umicore was recently notified by JP Morgan Chase & Co. that it has crossed the legal threshold of 3% for the direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments upwards on 27 April 2026. The total holding of direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments of JP Morgan Chase & Co. stands at 3.06% on 27 April 2026. Summary of the move: D

headline: JPMorgan Weighs Tokenized Treasury Pilot Against Sanctions Lawsuit Risks
summary: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) is participating in a cross border pilot to settle tokenized US Treasuries in near real time alongside Ripple, Mastercard, and Ondo Finance. The bank is also facing a lawsuit in China from HY Energy over the freezing of major fund transfers tied to US sanctions rules. For a global bank like JPMorgan Chase, this mix of digital asset settlement work and sanctions related scrutiny highlights how core banking activities are changing. Tokenized US Treasuries aim to move...

headline: It Takes Meta Almost 9 Minutes to Make $1 Million in Profits. Google Does It in Under 4
summary: The Number Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) reported Q1 FY2026 net income of $62.58 billion on April 29, 2026 via 8-K filing. That works out to roughly $1 million in profit every 3 minutes, 59 seconds. By contrast, Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) generated $26.77 billion in Q1 net income, or $1 million every 8 minutes, 42 seconds. Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) ... headline: How The LuxExperience B.V (NYSE:LUXE) Narrative Is Shifting After Share Count And Valuation Reset
summary: The latest update on LuxExperience B.V. comes with a lower fair value estimate, trimmed from $10.58 to $10.44, following refreshed modeling work. This has also influenced the rating shift from Overweight to Neutral and reset expectations around the stock’s risk and reward profile. As the story develops, you will see how these revisions feed into the evolving narrative and what to watch for next in the...

headline: How The Investment Story For EQT (EQT) Is Shifting With New Price Targets And Assumptions
summary: EQT's modeled fair value has been reset from $65.96 to $70.48, placing the stock closer to the upper end of recent analyst price target ranges. Many of these revisions are tied to different readings of commodity price assumptions, gas marketing execution, and how the market might value free cash flow and capital discipline. As you read on, you will see how to track these moving pieces and what they could mean for your own view on EQT. Stay updated as the Fair Value for EQT shifts by adding it...
